record.field: RSS-Bridge generates RSS and Atom feeds for websites that don't provide one, so any site can be followed from your feed reader.

RSS-Bridge generates RSS and Atom feeds for websites that don't provide one, so any site can be followed from your feed reader.

It is a lightweight PHP application (no database required) that uses purpose-built "bridges" plus generic CSS/XPath scrapers to turn pages from social networks, video platforms, blogs and news sites into clean, standards-compliant feeds. RSS-Bridge is not a feed reader itself; it sits between unfriendly websites and the reader or aggregator you already use, and can run as a web app or from the command line.

Key features
  • Hundreds of ready-made bridges for sites like YouTube, Reddit, Mastodon, Telegram, Twitch and many more
  • Generic CSS-selector and XPath bridges to build a feed from almost any HTML page
  • Multiple output formats: Atom, RSS/MRSS, JSON, HTML and plaintext
  • Pluggable cache backends (file, SQLite, Memcached) with no database dependency
  • Access control via token or HTTP Basic Auth, plus per-bridge enable/disable
  • Runs anywhere PHP runs: Docker, shared hosting, VPS or CLI
Best for

Self-hosters, journalists, researchers and power users who want to follow sites that dropped or never offered feeds, consolidate social and media sources into a single reader, and reclaim a private, algorithm-free way to track online content.

services: rss-bridge: image: rssbridge/rss-bridge:latest restart: unless-stopped expose: - 80 volumes: - ./rssbridge_config:/config container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
